summary.deploymentMode: Blue-green Catalyst candidate with the existing Slate site retained as rollback
summary.truthPolicy: Provider availability is reported by /api/v1/capabilities; this file describes targets, not live entitlement.

serviceMap.requiredService: Catalyst Web Client Hosting or Slate
serviceMap.prototypeEvidence: React/Vite build, route-level chunks, runtime badge, labeled read-only fallback.
Node 24 function with catalyst-config.json, validation, tests, security headers and /api/v1 contract.
Server enforcement and adapters are implemented; console mapping and invited users require live verification.
Normalized schema and Data Store adapter are implemented; tables must be provisioned before enabling.
File validation, hash and extraction are implemented; storage remains unavailable until a real bucket/folder passes verification.
Supervisor report endpoint uses SmartBrowz only when enabled and otherwise returns a labeled browser-print fallback.
Optional adapter boundary. Deterministic retrieval remains the core and no model response is simulated.
Server-only adapter uses cited evidence, four answer modes, bounded conversation context, uncited-FIR rejection, and deterministic fallback.
Optional adapter boundary; synchronous bounded analysis remains available.
Pipeline installs Node 24 dependencies, lints, builds, tests, evaluates and deploys through Catalyst CLI.

githubFlow.name: Validate feature branch
githubFlow.detail: Run npm run validate and both production dependency audits.

githubFlow.name: Deploy Catalyst Development candidate
githubFlow.detail: Bind the existing project, configure environment values and deploy the Advanced I/O function plus dist client.

githubFlow.name: Verify providers
githubFlow.detail: Enable each capability flag only after its real service call succeeds.

githubFlow.name: Promote and then update Slate
githubFlow.detail: Promote the separate candidate, retain rollback, and configure Slate to build dist only after acceptance.

readinessGates: Synthetic data only; no real police or citizen PII.
The deployed health path returns JSON rather than the SPA.
Every supported answer includes valid citations and refusal queries include none.
Roles are derived server-side and privileged endpoints reject unauthorized sessions.
Provider badges reflect runtime evidence rather than design intent.
The current Slate deployment remains untouched until the Catalyst candidate passes the judge journey.
